# Stacks Protocol 1.0-draft requirement catalog

Catalog revision 1. Core: 34 · Standard: 8 · Complete: 5.

Profile inheritance is `Core ← Standard ← Complete`. Security requirements are
part of Core. Only `pass` satisfies a required profile entry; failed, skipped,
unsupported, exception, and experimental results do not.

| ID | Profile | Level | Normative source | Evidence | Requirement |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `CORE-CONV-01` | Core | MUST | [conventions](https://github.com/stacksjs/white-paper/blob/main/README.md#3-project-conventions-and-layout) | behavior | Canonical application roles map to documented paths or language-idiomatic equivalents. |
| `CORE-CONV-02` | Core | MUST | [conventions](https://github.com/stacksjs/white-paper/blob/main/README.md#3-project-conventions-and-layout) | behavior | User-owned application code takes precedence over framework defaults at the same logical path. |
| `CORE-CONV-03` | Core | MUST | [conventions](https://github.com/stacksjs/white-paper/blob/main/README.md#3-project-conventions-and-layout) | inspection | Discovery and registration rules are deterministic and documented, including negative cases. |
| `CORE-CONV-04` | Core | MUST | [conventions](https://github.com/stacksjs/white-paper/blob/main/README.md#3-project-conventions-and-layout) | inspection | Naming rules for routes, tables, keys, environment variables, jobs, and generated artifacts are deterministic. |
| `CORE-CONV-05` | Core | MUST | [conventions](https://github.com/stacksjs/white-paper/blob/main/README.md#3-project-conventions-and-layout) | behavior | A scaffolded application starts in documented local-development mode after declared prerequisites are installed. |
| `CORE-MVA-01` | Core | MUST | [mva](https://github.com/stacksjs/white-paper/blob/main/README.md#2-modelviewaction) | behavior | An Action is invokable independently of HTTP or another exposing transport. |
| `CORE-MVA-02` | Core | MUST NOT | [mva](https://github.com/stacksjs/white-paper/blob/main/README.md#2-modelviewaction) | inspection | A Model depends on neither an HTTP request nor a UI renderer. |
| `CORE-GEN-01` | Core | MUST | [generation](https://github.com/stacksjs/white-paper/blob/main/README.md#22-derivation-and-generated-artifacts) | behavior | Generated migrations are reviewable before application and identify destructive changes. |
| `CORE-GEN-02` | Core | MUST | [generation](https://github.com/stacksjs/white-paper/blob/main/README.md#22-derivation-and-generated-artifacts) | behavior | Generated artifacts are deterministic for identical input and tool versions. |
| `CORE-GEN-03` | Core | MUST | [generation](https://github.com/stacksjs/white-paper/blob/main/README.md#22-derivation-and-generated-artifacts) | inspection | Generated artifacts identify their source revision and generator version or an equivalent provenance manifest. |
| `CORE-ERR-01` | Core | MUST | [errors](https://github.com/stacksjs/white-paper/blob/main/README.md#23-error-taxonomy) | behavior | Machine-readable adapters return stable error envelopes containing category or code, message, status, and optional field errors. |
| `CORE-ERR-02` | Core | MUST NOT | [errors](https://github.com/stacksjs/white-paper/blob/main/README.md#23-error-taxonomy) | behavior | Production errors expose stack traces, credentials, raw queries, or filesystem paths. |
| `CORE-ROUTE-01` | Core | MUST | [routing](https://github.com/stacksjs/white-paper/blob/main/README.md#41-routing) | behavior | Method and path matching, parameters, groups, middleware attachment, and named routes behave deterministically. |
| `CORE-ROUTE-02` | Core | MUST | [routing](https://github.com/stacksjs/white-paper/blob/main/README.md#41-routing) | behavior | Named routes reverse-generate URLs with validated required parameters. |
| `CORE-LIFE-01` | Core | MUST | [lifecycle](https://github.com/stacksjs/white-paper/blob/main/README.md#42-request-lifecycle) | behavior | Observable request execution preserves middleware, validation, authorization, Action, serialization or rendering, and response order. |
| `CORE-LIFE-02` | Core | MUST NOT | [lifecycle](https://github.com/stacksjs/white-paper/blob/main/README.md#42-request-lifecycle) | behavior | An Action executes after middleware, validation, or authorization produces a terminal response. |
| `CORE-VAL-01` | Core | MUST | [validation](https://github.com/stacksjs/white-paper/blob/main/README.md#43-validation) | behavior | Validation produces stable field-keyed errors for nested invalid input. |
| `CORE-VAL-02` | Core | MUST | [validation](https://github.com/stacksjs/white-paper/blob/main/README.md#43-validation) | behavior | Validation rejects invalid input before Action execution and documents any coercion. |
| `CORE-DATA-01` | Core | MUST | [data](https://github.com/stacksjs/white-paper/blob/main/README.md#44-data-and-persistence) | behavior | At least one persistence adapter supports CRUD, filtering, ordering, and pagination. |
| `CORE-DATA-02` | Core | MUST | [data](https://github.com/stacksjs/white-paper/blob/main/README.md#44-data-and-persistence) | behavior | Transactions roll back all writes when the transactional operation fails. |
| `CORE-DATA-03` | Core | MUST | [data](https://github.com/stacksjs/white-paper/blob/main/README.md#44-data-and-persistence) | behavior | Supported relationship and eager-loading behavior is reported per tested database dialect. |
| `CORE-MIG-01` | Core | MUST | [migrations](https://github.com/stacksjs/white-paper/blob/main/README.md#44-data-and-persistence) | behavior | Migrations apply in deterministic order and are reversible or explicitly compensating. |
| `CORE-MIG-02` | Core | MUST | [migrations](https://github.com/stacksjs/white-paper/blob/main/README.md#44-data-and-persistence) | behavior | Destructive migration operations are visible in a plan or preview before production application. |
| `CORE-CONFIG-01` | Core | MUST | [configuration](https://github.com/stacksjs/white-paper/blob/main/README.md#45-configuration) | behavior | Defaults, files, environment overrides, and explicit overrides have deterministic documented precedence. |
| `CORE-CONFIG-02` | Core | MUST | [configuration](https://github.com/stacksjs/white-paper/blob/main/README.md#45-configuration) | behavior | Invalid selected capabilities and missing required credentials fail clearly before unsafe work begins. |
| `CORE-CONFIG-03` | Core | MUST NOT | [configuration](https://github.com/stacksjs/white-paper/blob/main/README.md#45-configuration) | inspection | Secrets are embedded in generated client bundles. |
| `CORE-SEC-01` | Core | MUST | [security](https://github.com/stacksjs/white-paper/blob/main/README.md#56-security-baseline) | behavior | Database operations parameterize untrusted values by default. |
| `CORE-SEC-02` | Core | MUST | [security](https://github.com/stacksjs/white-paper/blob/main/README.md#56-security-baseline) | behavior | Rendered untrusted values are escaped by default. |
| `CORE-SEC-03` | Core | MUST | [security](https://github.com/stacksjs/white-paper/blob/main/README.md#56-security-baseline) | behavior | Applicable browser-authenticated writes reject missing or invalid CSRF tokens. |
| `CORE-SEC-04` | Core | MUST | [security](https://github.com/stacksjs/white-paper/blob/main/README.md#56-security-baseline) | inspection | Security headers are configurable and documented. |
| `CORE-SEC-05` | Core | MUST | [security](https://github.com/stacksjs/white-paper/blob/main/README.md#56-security-baseline) | behavior | Application encryption helpers provide authenticated encryption and reject modified ciphertext. |
| `CORE-SEC-06` | Core | MUST | [security](https://github.com/stacksjs/white-paper/blob/main/README.md#56-security-baseline) | inspection | Passwords use an algorithm intended for password storage and configurable work factors. |
| `CORE-SEC-07` | Core | MUST | [security](https://github.com/stacksjs/white-paper/blob/main/README.md#56-security-baseline) | behavior | Authenticator comparison is timing-safe where applicable. |
| `CORE-SEC-08` | Core | MUST | [security](https://github.com/stacksjs/white-paper/blob/main/README.md#56-security-baseline) | inspection | Trust boundaries, storage assumptions, key lifecycle, and security non-guarantees are documented. |
| `STD-AUTH-01` | Standard | MUST | [auth](https://github.com/stacksjs/white-paper/blob/main/README.md#46-identity-authentication-and-authorization) | behavior | Identity establishment, authorization gates, token or session revocation, and storage assumptions are tested. |
| `STD-QUEUE-01` | Standard | MUST | [queue](https://github.com/stacksjs/white-paper/blob/main/README.md#51-queues-and-scheduling) | behavior | At least one non-inline queue driver persists and executes named serialized Jobs with retries, timeouts, and failure recording. |
| `STD-DRV-01` | Standard | MUST | [drivers](https://github.com/stacksjs/white-paper/blob/main/README.md#47-driver-contract) | behavior | Unsupported or planned drivers fail loudly without fallback or silent degradation. |
| `STD-DRV-02` | Standard | MUST | [drivers](https://github.com/stacksjs/white-paper/blob/main/README.md#47-driver-contract) | inspection | Each supported driver is reported separately with runtime, platform, topology, provider version, and evidence revision. |
| `STD-OBS-01` | Standard | MUST | [observability](https://github.com/stacksjs/white-paper/blob/main/README.md#54-observability) | behavior | A request or trace identifier propagates through structured logs into at least one background-work path. |
| `STD-OBS-02` | Standard | MUST | [observability](https://github.com/stacksjs/white-paper/blob/main/README.md#54-observability) | behavior | Liveness and readiness surfaces distinguish process health from configured dependency readiness. |
| `STD-TYPE-01` | Standard | MUST | [types](https://github.com/stacksjs/white-paper/blob/main/README.md#57-type-evidence) | inspection | The implementation publishes checked shape evidence and identifies inferred, generated, cast, and runtime-only boundaries. |
| `STD-API-01` | Standard | MUST | [api](https://github.com/stacksjs/white-paper/blob/main/README.md#48-api-adapter) | behavior | A machine-readable API adapter applies validation, stable serialization, status codes, and the protocol error envelope. |
| `COMP-VIEW-01` | Complete | MUST | [views](https://github.com/stacksjs/white-paper/blob/main/README.md#21-roles) | behavior | A rendered-view adapter projects prepared data while preserving output escaping and authorization boundaries. |
| `COMP-API-01` | Complete | MUST | [api](https://github.com/stacksjs/white-paper/blob/main/README.md#48-api-adapter) | behavior | Machine-readable descriptions generated from registered routes and schemas are fresh and non-placeholder. |
| `COMP-RT-01` | Complete | MUST | [realtime](https://github.com/stacksjs/white-paper/blob/main/README.md#52-real-time-messaging) | behavior | Bidirectional events support public and authorization-gated channels, bounded payloads, rate limits, and documented scale-out behavior. |
| `COMP-NOTIFY-01` | Complete | MUST | [notifications](https://github.com/stacksjs/white-paper/blob/main/README.md#53-notifications) | behavior | A notification abstraction has at least two tested channels and isolates credentials and delivery failures from domain logic. |
| `COMP-DEPLOY-01` | Complete | MUST | [deployment](https://github.com/stacksjs/white-paper/blob/main/README.md#55-infrastructure-and-deployment) | behavior | At least one deployment adapter produces a reviewable plan and evidence for its tested provider and topology. |

## Independent extension badges

Extensions do not change the Core, Standard, or Complete result.

| ID | Extension | Required evidence |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `EXT-AI-01` | AI | provider contract; limitations; privacy boundary |
| `EXT-ANALYTICS-01` | Analytics | collection contract; reporting evidence; privacy model |
| `EXT-DESKTOP-01` | Desktop | packaged artifact; platform matrix; signing and update evidence |
| `EXT-CMS-01` | CMS | content lifecycle; authorization; rendering evidence |
| `EXT-COMMERCE-01` | Commerce | order lifecycle; payment boundary; failure evidence |
| `EXT-SEARCH-01` | Search | index contract; query contract; tested driver |
| `EXT-I18N-01` | Internationalization | catalog contract; locale negotiation; formatting evidence |
